title: 10 best herbal teas for high cholesterol
intro_desc: Herbal teas for high cholesterol are caffeine-free beverages made from various plants, flowers, and spices, which can help manage high cholesterol levels. These teas often contain beneficial compounds that inhibit cholesterol absorption, increase bile excretion, and promote overall heart health. Some popular herbal teas for high cholesterol include hawthorn, holy basil, and black or green tea. For example, hawthorn tea is rich in antioxidants that help reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol, while holy basil tea has anti-inflammatory properties that may lower triglycerides and cholesterol levels. Regularly consuming these teas can contribute to improved heart health, reduced risk of cardiovascular diseases, and an overall enhanced quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: White tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains high levels of antioxidants, particularly catechins, which have been shown to inhibit the absorption of cholesterol in the body. These antioxidants also help to reduce the oxidative stress that can damage blood vessels and lead to plaque buildup. Furthermore, white tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the arteries and improve overall heart health. Regular consumption of white tea may help to lower both LDL (bad) cholesterol and total cholesterol levels, reducing the risk of heart disease and other complications associated with high cholesterol.
remedy_properties: Anti-oxidative properties: White tea contains high levels of antioxidants, such as flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, thereby preventing the oxidation of LDL cholesterol and reducing the risk of plaque buildup in the arteries.

Anti-inflammatory properties: White tea has potent anti-inflammatory effects, which can help to reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby reducing the risk of inflammation-induced plaque buildup and lowering cholesterol levels.

Anti-atherogenic properties: White tea has been shown to inhibit the formation of atherosclerotic plaques, which are caused by the buildup of cholesterol and other substances in the arteries, by reducing the oxidation of LDL cholesterol and preventing the formation of foam cells.

Hypolipidemic properties: White tea has been found to have hypolipidemic effects, which means it can help to lower lipid levels in the blood, including cholesterol and triglycerides, by increasing the breakdown and excretion of cholesterol.

Vasodilatory properties: White tea has vasodilatory effects, which means it can help to widen blood vessels and improve blood flow, thereby reducing blood pressure and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease, which is often associated with high cholesterol levels. The active constituents responsible for these properties include catechins, theaflavins, thearubigins, and other polyphenols.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Large-leaved lime tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains high levels of antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels. The tea is also rich in vitamin C, which helps to reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, both of which can contribute to high cholesterol. Additionally, large-leaved lime tea has been shown to inhibit the absorption of cholesterol in the gut, further helping to lower cholesterol levels. Drinking large-leaved lime tea regularly can help to manage high cholesterol levels and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Hypolipidemic: Large-leaved lime tea helps lower cholesterol levels by reducing the amount of lipids (fats) in the blood, thanks to its active constituents, such as flavonoids and triterpenoids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of large-leaved lime tea help protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels, due to the presence of various polyphenols, including flavonoids and tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory effects of large-leaved lime tea can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which is associated with high cholesterol, as a result of active compounds like limonoids and flavonoids.

Hepatoprotective: Large-leaved lime tea supports liver health, which is crucial for managing cholesterol levels, because it contains hepatoprotective compounds like terpenoids and flavonoids that help maintain proper liver function.

Vasodilatory: The vasodilatory properties of large-leaved lime tea help widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of cholesterol plaque buildup, due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains compounds that inhibit the absorption of cholesterol in the intestines. This action helps to reduce the overall levels of cholesterol in the body, thereby promoting cardiovascular health. The tea is rich in antioxidants that protect the cells from damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of high cholesterol. Additionally, maidenhair tree tea has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the arteries, allowing for better blood flow and reducing the risk of heart disease. Drinking maidenhair tree tea regularly can help manage high cholesterol levels and promote overall cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Hypolipidemic: Maidenhair tree tea helps lower high cholesterol levels by reducing lipid concentrations, thanks to its active constituents, such as ginkgolides and bilobalide.
Antioxidant: Its rich antioxidant properties, due to high flavonoid content, protect the cardiovascular system by preventing oxidative damage to LDL cholesterol particles.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory effects of maidenhair tree tea, attributed to ginkgolides, help alleviate inflammation in blood vessels, promoting better blood flow and reducing the risk of plaque buildup.

Platelet aggregation inhibition: Maidenhair tree tea inhibits platelet aggregation, preventing the formation of blood clots, which is crucial for maintaining healthy blood circulation and lowering the risk of cardiovascular diseases associated with high cholesterol.
Vasodilatory: The vasodilatory effects of the tea, caused by terpene lactones, widen blood vessels, improving blood flow and reducing the risk of hypertension and associated complications.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cat's claw tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties that may support heart health. The herb, Uncaria tomentosa, from which cat's claw tea is derived, has been shown to reduce the levels of low-density lipoprotein (LDL), or "bad" cholesterol, and increase high-density lipoprotein (HDL), or "good" cholesterol. Moreover, cat's claw tea may help prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol, which can lead to plaque buildup in the arteries and increase the risk of heart disease. By promoting healthy cholesterol levels and reducing inflammation, cat's claw tea can contribute to overall cardiovascular health and well-being.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Cat's claw tea's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce inflammation in the body, which is important for high cholesterol as chronic inflammation can contribute to the development and progression of atherosclerosis, a condition characterized by plaque buildup in the arteries. The active constituents responsible for these effects are thought to be a group of oxindole alkaloids.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of cat's claw tea help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which is important for high cholesterol as oxidized LDL cholesterol can contribute to the formation of plaque in the arteries. The antioxidant compounds found in cat's claw include tannins, flavonoids, and sterols.

Immune-boosting: Cat's claw tea can help strengthen the immune system, which is important for high cholesterol as a strong immune system can help the body better manage inflammation and oxidative stress. The immune-boosting properties of cat's claw are attributed to its alkaloids, particularly pentacylic oxindole alkaloids.

Antihypertensive: Cat's claw tea has been shown to have a mild antihypertensive effect, which is important for high cholesterol as high blood pressure can damage the arteries and contribute to the development of atherosclerosis. The active constituents responsible for this effect are thought to be the alkaloids and glycosides found in cat's claw.

Hypolipidemic: Cat's claw tea has been shown to have hypolipidemic effects, which means it can help lower levels of fats in the blood, including cholesterol. This is important for high cholesterol as high levels of cholesterol in the blood can increase the risk of heart disease. The active constituents responsible for these effects are thought to be the sterols and triterpenes found in cat's claw.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains allicin, a compound that has been shown to help lower cholesterol levels. Allicin is known to reduce the production of cholesterol in the liver and decrease the absorption of cholesterol in the intestines. Additionally, garlic tea has antioxidant properties that can help protect the cardiovascular system from damage caused by high cholesterol. The tea also has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the arteries, which can contribute to the development of high cholesterol. Drinking garlic tea regularly may help lower cholesterol levels and promote overall heart health.
remedy_properties: Hypolipidemic: Garlic tea has been shown to lower levels of total cholesterol, LDL cholesterol, and triglycerides in the blood, which is important for reducing the risk of plaque buildup in arteries and decreasing the risk of heart disease. This property is attributed to the active constituents, allicin and ajoene, which inhibit the production of cholesterol in the liver.

Antioxidant: Garlic tea contains antioxidants that help prevent the oxidation of LDL cholesterol, which is important for reducing the risk of plaque formation in arteries and decreasing the risk of heart disease. The antioxidant properties come from various compounds, including allicin, allyl sulfides, and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Garlic tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which is important for preventing the progression of atherosclerosis and decreasing the risk of heart disease. The anti-inflammatory effects are attributed to the active constituents, allicin and diallyl disulfide.

Fibrinolytic: Garlic tea has fibrinolytic properties that help prevent the formation of blood clots, which is important for reducing the risk of heart attack and stroke. The fibrinolytic activity is due to the presence of allicin and ajoene, which inhibit platelet aggregation and enhance fibrinolysis.

Vasodilatory: Garlic tea has vasodilatory properties that help widen blood vessels, which is important for improving blood flow and reducing blood pressure. The vasodilatory effects are attributed to the active constituents, allicin and nitric oxide, which relax smooth muscle in blood vessel walls.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cinnamon tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains antioxidants and properties that can help lower levels of LDL (bad) cholesterol and triglycerides, while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol. The compound called cinnamaldehyde in cinnamon is thought to inhibit the production of cholesterol in the body. Additionally, cinnamon is rich in fiber, which can bind to bile acids and help remove them from the body, thereby reducing cholesterol levels. Regular consumption of cinnamon tea may contribute to maintaining healthy cholesterol levels and supporting cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Cinnamon tea contains antioxidants, such as polyphenols, which help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, contributing to improved lipid profiles and reduced cholesterol levels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory effects of cinnamon tea, attributed to compounds like cinnamaldehyde, help decrease inflammation in the body, reducing the risk of plaque buildup in the arteries and high cholesterol.

Hypoglycemic properties: Cinnamon tea has been shown to lower blood sugar levels, which is important for high cholesterol as high blood sugar can exacerbate cholesterol issues and increase the risk of heart disease.
Lipid-lowering properties: Cinnamon tea can help reduce triglyceride and LDL ("bad") cholesterol levels, while increasing HDL ("good") cholesterol levels, promoting overall heart health.
Blood thinning properties: Cinnamon tea has blood-thinning effects, which can help prevent blood clots and reduce the risk of heart attacks and strokes, both of which are linked to high cholesterol.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Turmeric tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains curcumin, a powerful antioxidant that has been shown to lower levels of bad cholesterol (LDL) and increase good cholesterol (HDL) in the body. Curcumin works by reducing inflammation and preventing the oxidation of LDL cholesterol, which can lead to plaque buildup in the arteries. Additionally, turmeric tea has been found to improve liver function, which plays a crucial role in removing cholesterol from the body. By incorporating turmeric tea into a healthy lifestyle, individuals with high cholesterol may be able to reduce their risk of heart disease and other related health conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Turmeric tea's anti-inflammatory property, due to the active constituent curcumin, helps reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels.
Antioxidant: The high antioxidant content of turmeric tea, primarily from curcumin, aids in preventing oxidative stress and damage to cells, including those that affect cholesterol metabolism.
Lowering LDL cholesterol: Turmeric tea has been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels, thanks to curcumin's ability to inhibit the absorption of cholesterol in the intestines.
Increasing HDL cholesterol: Curcumin in turmeric tea can increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels, helping improve the overall cholesterol balance in the body.

Anti-atherosclerotic: The anti-atherosclerotic property of turmeric tea, attributed to curcumin, helps prevent the formation of plaque in the arteries, reducing the risk of cardiovascular diseases associated with high cholesterol.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to lower cholesterol levels. This compound works by preventing the absorption of cholesterol in the gut, thus reducing the amount of cholesterol that enters the bloodstream. Additionally, licorice tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce inflammation in the arteries and improve blood flow. This can help prevent the buildup of plaque in the arteries, which is a major contributor to high cholesterol. Furthermore, licorice tea has been shown to increase the production of bile in the liver, which can help remove cholesterol from the body. Overall, licorice tea can be a natural and effective way to help lower cholesterol levels and improve cardiovascular health.
remedy_properties: Cholesterol-lowering effect: Licorice tea contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which have been shown to help reduce LDL (bad) cholesterol levels and increase HDL (good) cholesterol levels.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Licorice tea has anti-inflammatory effects due to its active constituents, including flavonoids and coumarins, which can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, thereby alleviating high cholesterol-induced damage.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice tea contains potent antioxidants, such as liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin, which can help protect the cardiovascular system from oxidative stress and reduce the oxidation of LDL cholesterol, a key factor in the development of high cholesterol.

Hepatoprotective effects: Licorice tea has been shown to have hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect the liver from damage caused by high cholesterol levels. The active constituents responsible for this effect include glycyrrhizin and its derivatives.

Blood sugar regulation: Licorice tea may help regulate blood sugar levels, which can indirectly help manage high cholesterol. The active constituents responsible for this effect include amorfrutins and glabridin, which have been shown to improve insulin sensitivity and reduce blood glucose levels.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roselle tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains high levels of antioxidants, particularly anthocyanins, which have been shown to lower cholesterol levels. The tea is also rich in vitamin C, calcium, and iron, which can help to boost the immune system and improve overall health. Additionally, roselle tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the body and improve cardiovascular health. Studies have shown that regular consumption of roselle tea can help to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels, while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels. This makes roselle tea a natural and effective way to help manage high cholesterol and promote heart health.
remedy_properties: Hypolipidemic effect: Roselle tea helps lower high cholesterol levels by inhibiting the absorption of cholesterol and increasing the excretion of bile acids, attributed mainly to its anthocyanin and flavonoid content.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in roselle tea, such as vitamin C, anthocyanins, and polyphenols, protect LDL cholesterol from oxidation, which contributes to atherosclerosis, a condition linked to high cholesterol.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory effects of roselle tea, due to its richness in anthocyanins and other polyphenols, help alleviate inflammation in blood vessels, reducing the risk of atherosclerosis and cardiovascular issues associated with high cholesterol.

Vasodilatory properties: Roselle tea contains compounds like anthocyanins and hibiscus acid that promote vasodilation, improving blood flow and reducing the strain on the cardiovascular system, which is crucial for managing high cholesterol.

Diuretic properties: Roselle tea increases urination, aiding in the removal of excess fluids and sodium from the body, which can help lower blood pressure and, consequently, high cholesterol levels, thanks to its organic acids and polyphenols content.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm tea helps with high cholesterol because it contains a unique antioxidant called rosmarinic acid, which has been shown to lower LDL (bad) cholesterol levels while increasing HDL (good) cholesterol levels. Additionally, lemon balm has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the arteries, which can contribute to high cholesterol. The tea's calming effects may also help reduce stress, which is a known contributor to high cholesterol. Furthermore, lemon balm tea has been shown to improve blood flow and circulation, which can help prevent the buildup of plaque in the arteries. Overall, drinking lemon balm tea can be a natural and effective way to help manage high cholesterol levels.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemon balm tea contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels. The active constituents responsible for these properties include rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Hypolipidemic properties: Lemon balm tea has been shown to lower lipid levels, including LDL (bad) cholesterol and triglycerides. This is due to the presence of eugenol, a compound that inhibits cholesterol synthesis.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lemon balm tea has anti-inflammatory effects that can help reduce inflammation in the cardiovascular system, which can contribute to high cholesterol levels. The active constituents responsible for these properties include rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Antihypertensive properties: Lemon balm tea has been shown to lower blood pressure, which can help reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease in individuals with high cholesterol. The active constituents responsible for these properties include rosmarinic acid and eugenol.

Antianxiety properties: Lemon balm tea has been shown to have calming effects, which can help reduce stress and anxiety. This is important for high cholesterol because stress and anxiety can contribute to unhealthy lifestyle habits, such as overeating and sedentary behavior, which can increase cholesterol levels. The active constituents responsible for these properties include rosmarinic acid and citral.
